Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class FlowActionCall

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.FlowBaseElement
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.FlowElement
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.FlowNode
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.FlowActionCall
public class FlowActionCall extends FlowNode

Java class for FlowActionCall complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="FlowActionCall">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowNode">
       <sequence>
         <element name="actionName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="actionType" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}InvocableActionType" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="connector" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowConnector" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="dataTypeMappings" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowDataTypeMapping" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="faultConnector" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowConnector" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="flowTransactionModel" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowTransactionModel" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="inputParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowActionCallInputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="nameSegment" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="outputParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowActionCallOutputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="storeOutputAutomatically" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="versionSegment" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}int" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

      value - allowed object is FlowConnector

    • getDataTypeMappings

      public List<FlowDataTypeMapping> getDataTypeMappings()
      Gets the value of the dataTypeMappings property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the dataTypeMappings property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getDataTypeMappings().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowDataTypeMapping

      Returns:
      The value of the dataTypeMappings property.

    • getFaultConnector

      public FlowConnector getFaultConnector()
      Gets the value of the faultConnector property.
      Returns:
      possible object is FlowConnector

      Sets the value of the faultConnector property.

      Sets the value of the flowTransactionModel property.

      value - allowed object is FlowTransactionModel

    • getInputParameters

      public List<FlowActionCallInputParameter> getInputParameters()
      Gets the value of the inputParameters property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the inputParameters property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getInputParameters().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowActionCallInputParameter

      Returns:
      The value of the inputParameters property.

      public List<FlowActionCallOutputParameter> getOutputParameters()

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the outputParameters property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getOutputParameters().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowActionCallOutputParameter

      Returns:
      The value of the outputParameters property.
